assumptions: The dividend receivable/payable is a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup monetary item.
The stated exchange exposure means the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable is relevant to the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of certain intragroup monetary items to be designated in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The cash flow hedge model is also relevant because IFRS 9 applies it to foreign currency risk associated with recognised assets or liabilities when the variability can affect profit or loss.
